STT 1, 014 [SB Gilgameš 07][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
oo  (traces of 1 line)
o 2'2'

[... u₂]-⸢bu⸣-[ki]

(Enkidu speaks:) '[...] your thickness,

o 3'3'

[...] ⸢ša₂ iš⸣-ten₂-⸢ma⸣

'[...] are from a single (piece);

o 4'4'

[...] ⸢e⸣-la-⸢niš ar⸣-ti-⸢te?⸣-<<U>>-⸢ka⸣

'[...] I hung you up.

o 5'5'

[... giš]⸢IG ki-i⸣ an-nu-u [gi-mil]-⸢ki? :⸣ KI.MIN<([...)> giš]⸢IG ki-i⸣ an-nu-u ⸢du-muq-ki⸣

'[...], o door, that this would be your reward, : ditto ([...], o door, that this) would be your bounty,

o 6'6'

[x] ⸢aš₂-ši⸣ [...] lu ak-ki!(KU-)sa ⸢ka⸣-[a-ši ...] ⸢lu u₂-šar-ki⸣-ba [a]-⸢na⸣ E₂.⸢BABBAR⸣.[RA]

'I [...] have picked up [...], I would have cut you down, [...] I would have shipped you [...] to Ebabbara.

o 7'7'

[x] ⸢E₂⸣.BABBAR.⸢RA⸣ E₂ dša₂-maš lu u₂-⸢ša₂⸣-[bi?-lak?-ki? ...] ⸢E₂⸣.BABBAR.RA giš⸢EREN lu-u₂ az-qup⸣

'[...] Ebabbara, the temple of Šamaš, I would have brought you, [...] Ebabbara I would have set up the cedar.

o 8'8'

[x] KA₂-šu₂ ⸢lu-u uš⸣-zi-za an-⸢za⸣-[a ...] x x ne₂-re-bi-ki ⸢lu⸣-[u x x]

'[...] its gate I would have stationed Anzu [...] ... your entrance I would have [...];

o 9'9'

⸢lu⸣-u am-x [x (x)] x x [x] ⸢ša₂⸣ IRI [...] dša₂-[maš]

'I would have ... [...] ... [...] of the city [...] Šamaš,

o 10'10'

⸢u₃⸣ ina ⸢UNUGki⸣ x x ⸢I⸣ KI x [...] x [(x)]

'and in Uruk ... [...] ...;

o 11'11'

[aš₂]-⸢šu₂ dša₂⸣-maš iš-⸢mu⸣-u qa-ba-⸢a-a :⸣ ina PI? x [...] x RI giš⸢TUKUL it⸣-[tan?]-⸢na?⸣

'because Šamaš heard what I said, : in ... [...] ... he gave me a weapon.

o 12'12'

e-nen-na giš⸢IG ana-ku⸣ e-pu-uš-ki ana-ku aš₂-⸢šak⸣-[ki ... ana]-⸢ku⸣ a-na-⸢as⸣-saḫ-ki

'Now, o door, it was I who made you, it was I who raised you up! [...], can I tear you out?

o 13'13'

lu-u LUGAL ša₂ e-la-a EGIR-ia₂ li-<ze>-er-ki : lu-u DINGIR x [...] x [(x)]-ša₂ ⸢li-ir⸣-te-ki-⸢ma⸣

'May either a king who rises after me loathe you, : or may a god ... [...] ... [(...)] ... hang you up.

o 14'14'

šu-mi li-na-ak-⸢ki-ir⸣-ma šum-šu₂ liš-kun : ⸢it-ta⸣-[saḫ ...] x-⸢ma?⸣ id-⸢di⸣

'May he alter my name and put his name.' : He tore out [...] ... he threw down.

o 15'15'

a-ma-ti-šu₂ iš-⸢te⸣-nem-ma-a ḫa-an-ṭiš ḫar-piš ⸢il⸣-[la-ka di-ma-a]-šu₂

He was listening carefully to his words, quickly, early his tears were flowing.

o 16'16'

dGIŠ-⸢gim₂⸣-maš a-ma-ti ša₂ den-ki-du₃ ib-ri-šu₂ iš-te-⸢nem⸣-[ma-a ... il-la]-ka di-ma-a-[šu₂]

Gilgameš was listening carefully to the words of Enkidu, his friend, [...] his tears were flowing.

o 17'17'

⸢dGIŠ-gim₂⸣-maš KA-šu₂ DU₃uš-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra den-ki-⸢du₃⸣ [...] x šu-pu-u

Gilgameš opened his mouth to speak, saying to Enkidu: '[...] ... manifest,

o 18'18'

[...] ⸢GEŠTU?⸣-MIN? ṭe₃-mu ra-šu-u₂ ša₂-na-ti-ma [...] x x [...]

'[...] possesses wisdom, good judgement, [...] ... [...] hostilities?

o 19'19'

⸢am-mi⸣-ni ib-ri id-bu-ub lib₃-ba-[ka ...]

'Why, my friend, did your heart utter [...]?

o 20'20'

[...] ⸢šu⸣-qu-rat-ma pi-rit-tu₄ ma-⸢aʾ⸣-da-at [... ki]-⸢ma zu-um⸣-[bi]

'[...] was precious and the fear was much. [...] like flies,

o 21'21'

[...] ⸢ma⸣-aʾ-du šu-ut-tu₄ aq-rat : ana ⸢bal-ṭi⸣ [...] ⸢na-sa⸣-sa

'[...] were many, the dream was rare. : "To the survivor grieving [...],

o 22'22'

[mi-i?]-⸢tu₄⸣ ana bal-ṭi ni-is-sa-⸢ta i⸣-zib

"the deceased left grieving for the survivor."

o 23'23'

[lu-ṣal]-⸢la⸣-ma lu-sap-pa-a DINGIR-⸢MEŠ GAL⸣-MEŠ

'Let me beseech and let me supplicate the great gods,

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] lu-uš-te-eʾ-a ⸢i-lu-ka⸣ lu-us-ḫir [...] x x x [...]

'let me seek out [...], let me turn to your god. [...] ... [...],

r 22

[liš]-⸢mi⸣ dEN.LIL₂ ma-li-⸢ku GAL?⸣ ina IGI-⸢ka ŠUD₃?⸣-[ia₂? ...] ⸢lu?⸣-u ut-[nen?-ni? ...]

'may Ellil, the great counsellor, hear my prayers in your presence, [...] may my entreaty [...].

r 33

[KU₃].⸢SI₂₂⸣ la mi-na ṣa-lam-⸢ka⸣ lu-pu-uš : [...] x x x [...]

'Let me make your image of unlimited gold, : [...] ... [...].'

r 44

[... KU₃].BABBAR la ta-nam-din KU₃.SI₂₂ ⸢la ta⸣-x x x la [...]

'[...], do not give silver, do not ... gold, [...] not [...].

r 55

[... dEN].⸢LIL₂?⸣ iq-bu-u ul ki-i ša₂ ⸢DINGIR⸣-MEŠ ša₂ x [...]

'[...] Ellil commanded is not like that of the gods of ... [...].

r 66

[...] ⸢iq⸣-bu-u ul i-tur ul ⸢ip⸣-[šiṭ]

"[...] he commanded he did not erase again,

r 77

[...] ul?-te!(ŠI-)ed-du-u ul i-tur ul ip-šiṭ : ⸢ib-ri uṣ-ṣu⸣-[ra?] x [x]

"[...] he made known he did not erase again." : My friend, ... [...] is drawn,

r 88

⸢i⸣-[na] ⸢la⸣ NAM-MEŠ-ši-na UN-MEŠ il-⸢la⸣-ka : mim-mu-⸢u₂⸣ še-⸢e-ri⸣ ina [na]-⸢ma⸣-[ri]

'people do go to their fate before their time.' : At the first glimmer of dawn,

r 99

⸢iš-ši⸣ re-ši-šu den-ki-du₃ a-na pa-an ⸢dUTU⸣ i-⸢nam⸣-[bi]

Enkidu raised his head, lamenting before Šamaš,

r 1010

⸢a⸣-[na] ⸢pa⸣-an ša₂-ru-⸢ri⸣ ša₂ d⸢UTU⸣ il-la-⸢ka di-ma-a⸣-[šu]

his tears flowing before the light of Šamaš:

r 1111

[am]-⸢ḫur⸣-[ka ...] ⸢aš₂⸣-šu₂ aq-ra-⸢ti⸣ [na]-⸢piš-ti⸣-[ia]

'I appeal to you, [...], for the sake of my most precious life!

r 1212

[... ḫab-bi]-⸢la LU₂⸣ : ⸢ša₂⸣ la ⸢u₂-šam⸣-ṣa-[an-nu ...]

'[...] the trapper man, : who did not let me be a match [...],

r 1313

[... ḫul]-⸢liq?⸣ [...]

'Destroy [...]!

r 1414

[...] ina [...]

'[...] in [...],

r 1515

[... a]-⸢pa⸣-[ni-iš]

'[...] through the window.'

(1 line missing)
r 1717

[...] ⸢du-ur da⸣-[a-ar₂]

'[...] all eternity:

r 1818

[... liṭ]-⸢ḫu-ki ka⸣-[a-ši]

'[...] may they catch up with you.

r 1919

[...] x x [x] ⸢TI⸣ [x (x)]

r 2020

[... qaq]-⸢qa?⸣-[ru] ⸢li⸣-[šaḫ-ḫi]

'May the ground defile [...].

r 2121

[... tur-bu-uʾ]-⸢i li-bal⸣-[lil]

'May [...] smear with dust [...].

(traces of 1 line)
r 2323

[... tar-ši]-⸢i⸣

'[...] you [...] get [...].

r 2424

[... E₂]-⸢ki⸣

'[...] your house.

r 2525

[... mu-ša₂]-⸢bu⸣-ki

'[...] your sitting place.

r 2626

[... man-za]-⸢zu⸣-ki
